title: 7 Reasons God Hasn’t Answered Yet + One Move
hook: Prayed and nothing changed? Here are 7 biblical reasons—and the one move to make today.
script: Prayed and nothing changed? Here are 7 biblical reasons—and the one move to make today. 1) God's timing isn't here yet (Habakkuk 2:3). 2) Motives need refining (James 4:3). 3) Unconfessed sin is blocking joy (Psalm 66:18). 4) You're being invited to persevere (Luke 18:1). 5) There's spiritual resistance you can't see (Daniel 10:12-13). 6) God is redirecting your path (Proverbs 16:9). 7) He's growing your trust and character (James 1:3-4). One move: pray Psalm 139:23-24, ask which one applies, then write one small step of obedience and do it today. Finish with thanks, even before you see it. Repeat daily this week. Waiting is not wasting when God is working.

title: When God Feels Silent: 7 Reasons and a Reset
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. Silence isn't absence; it's often alignment. Sometimes the timing is still ripening (Habakkuk 2:3). Sometimes motives need purifying (Proverbs 16:2). Sometimes confession clears static (1 John 1:9). Sometimes perseverance muscles must grow (Matthew 7:7). Sometimes there's warfare around your answer (Ephesians 6:12). Sometimes closed doors are holy redirections (Acts 16:6-7). And sometimes grace says "My strength is enough" in weakness (2 Corinthians 12:9). One move: the 5-5-5 reset. Five minutes meditating on the Lord's Prayer (Matthew 6:9-13). Five minutes of quiet with the breath prayer, "Your will, not mine." Five minutes asking boldly, then obey one nudge. Do this seven days. Watch for small openings.

title: The 7-Part Prayer Check You Needed Today
hook: This truth can change how you see your struggle
script: This truth can change how you see your struggle: unanswered doesn't mean unwanted. Run this 7-part check in prayer: Time - God makes all things beautiful in its time (Ecclesiastes 3:11). Motive - He weighs the heart (James 4:3; Proverbs 16:2). Sin - own it and receive mercy (Psalm 66:18; 1 John 1:9). Persevere - keep knocking (Luke 18:1). Warfare - stand firm and pray (Ephesians 6:12, 18). Redirection - He guides steps, even through no's (Proverbs 16:9). Trust - lean not on your own understanding (Proverbs 3:5-6). One move today: pick one promise, Psalm 27:14 or Romans 8:28, write it, pray it twice daily for seven days, and tell one friend. Agreement breaks isolation; faith grows in the wait.
